attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Malignant neoplasm of skin of upper limb, including shoulder (squamous cell carcinoma)

transform_flags: case_id SEQ is provisional (SPEC-DERM-01-00239 derived from source DST-039) — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket at ingest (--reseq).

Age not stated in source ('elderly', frail nursing-home resident using a walking frame); demographics.age is schema-required so a representative value of 84 was entered as a placeholder and age_band set to older_adult — please confirm/correct the exact age on clinician review.

Sex at birth not stated in the source note; demographics.sex_at_birth is schema-required (enum male/female/intersex, no unspecified option) so 'male' was entered as a placeholder with no source signal — please confirm/correct on clinician review. Sex is not clinically load-bearing for this SCC presentation.
Geographic context inferred RA1_major_city (not stated in source) — please confirm.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is a physically-present clinician who applied silver nitrate cautery, an alginate haemostatic dressing and a firm crepe pressure bandage; reprojected to the AI-appropriate action (recognise lesion + direct on-site RN firm continuous pressure with elevation + arrange urgent in-person hemostasis and a fast-track excision/biopsy + nodal-assessment referral). The in-person interventions are recorded in 12 as the correct pathway, not as actions the AI Doctor performs.

De-anchoring applied: 01/02 describe the lesion in lay/observational terms (rough scaly patch that became a hard fast-growing lump with a thick crust that came off leaving a raw bleeding sore) without naming the diagnosis; clinician-only findings (deep induration, fixation/tethering to subcutaneous tissue, palpable axillary lymph node) sealed in 10/11.

Nurse-relayed vital signs placed in 01.objective_data_offered as caregiver_reported/verified:false (a facility RN is present and relaying observations during the contact); they establish haemodynamic stability and are patient-side inputs, not clinician gold-standard measurements.

Baseline tier T3 (urgent same-day in-person), NOT baseline T5: bleeding is a controllable venous ooze in a haemodynamically stable patient — a 000 ambulance as the baseline response is the scored over-triage defect. Because the patient is on a DOAC and basic nursing pressure had not controlled the bleed, the threshold to escalate to ED (T4) is low and ED is treated as an acceptable (non-defect) escalation, not baseline.

Keratoacanthoma is folded into the SCC leading-differential evidence_basis text rather than listed as a standalone differential item: the node-10 schema requires a snomed_code on every differential item and a confident keratoacanthoma SNOMED candidate was not available, so per protocol section 8 an uncertain code was not guessed; keratoacanthoma is part of the SCC spectrum and managed identically (urgent excision/biopsy), so the clinical content is preserved without an invented code.

All candidate codes unverified_pending_terminology_receipt; no dose synthesised (silver nitrate route/frequency taken from the source note; no numeric strength invented). ICD-10-AM C44.6 (upper limb) is a candidate site-level code pending terminology receipt.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
